## Test Results
### tests/shared/ - FAILED
``` ..................................F =================================== FAILURES =================================== __________ TestWorktreeCreationSchema.test_invalid_issue_number_type ___________ tests/shared/github/test_metadata_schemas.py:139: in test_invalid_issue_number_type schema.validate(data) packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/metadata/schemas.py:75: in validate raise ValueError("plan_number must be an integer") E ValueError: plan_number must be an integer
During handling of the above exception, another exception occurred: tests/shared/github/test_metadata_schemas.py:138: in test_invalid_issue_number_type with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="issue_number must be an integer"): ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E AssertionError: Regex pattern did not match. E Expected regex: 'issue_number must be an integer' E Actual message: 'plan_number must be an integer' =========================== short test summary info ============================ FAILED tests/shared/github/test_metadata_schemas.py::TestWorktreeCreationSchema::test_invalid_issue_number_type - AssertionError: Regex pattern did not match. Expected regex: 'issue_number must be an integer' Actual message: 'plan_number must be an integer' !!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!! stopping after 1 failures !!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!! ```
**Status:** 33 passed, 1 failed
**Issue:** The test expects an error message saying "issue_number must be an integer" but the code is raising "plan_number must be an integer". This indicates a mismatch between the test expectation and the current error message in the schema validation code.
---
### tests/tui/ - FAILED
``` .............................................................F =================================== FAILURES =================================== _________ TestCmuxFocusWorkspace.test_focus_selects_matching_workspace _________ tests/tui/app/test_async_operations.py:1172: in test_focus_selects_matching_workspace assert len(captured_run_calls) == 2 E assert 0 == 2 E + where 0 = len([]) =========================== short test summary info ============================ FAILED tests/tui/app/test_async_operations.py::TestCmuxFocusWorkspace::test_focus_selects_matching_workspace - assert 0 == 2 + where 0 = len([]) !!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!! stopping after 1 failures !!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!! ```
**Status:** 61 passed, 1 failed
**Issue:** The test expects 2 captured run calls but got 0. This suggests the mocked/captured subprocess calls are not being recorded as expected, likely indicating a change in how the focus operation interacts with the subprocess layer or how the mock is capturing calls.
